_comment: Ground truth for M1.3 STL import fixtures. Authored and committed by the human maintainer (fixture discipline, AGENT_PROMPTS §4). Agents assert against these values; they do not edit this file. Units: mm, mm^3. Volumes computed by exact signed-volume (divergence theorem) over the triangle mesh AS STORED ON DISK.
_erratum: 2026-07-04: sphere mesh_volume_mm3 corrected from 4047.044679978858 to 4047.044792044954. The original value was computed from full-precision vertices BEFORE the STL was written at 7 significant figures, so it was not reproducible from the committed file at the stated 1e-9 tolerance. Found by agent run 005 (handoff 005, Blocked); independently verified by the maintainer's reviewer. The value below is computed from the on-disk quantized vertices and is what any correct importer must reproduce.

sphere_r10mm.stl._note: Icosphere, radius 10, 2 subdivisions, vertices stored at 7 significant figures. Mesh volume is ~96.6% of the analytic sphere volume; this is inherent tessellation deficit, not an import error. Import tests must assert the MESH volume above (computed from the on-disk vertices), not the analytic one.

broken_open_cube.stl._note: The 10mm cube with its +X face (2 triangles) removed, leaving a 4-edge open boundary. The importer MUST reject this mesh with a diagnostic that identifies it as non-watertight/open. Its signed volume is undefined for an open mesh and must not be reported as valid.
